title: The Expedited Specialist pathway
metaTitle: Expedited Specialist pathway 2026: specialties, eligibility & steps
metaDescription: Australia's fast-track Expedited Specialist pathway for overseas specialists: which specialties are included (updated 2026), what you skip, the 6 months supervised practice, and how to check the accepted qualifications list.
summaryMarkdown: The Expedited Specialist pathway is the newest and fastest route to specialist registration. It's for specialist international medical graduates (SIMGs) whose qualification is on the Medical Board's *Expedited Specialist pathway: accepted qualifications list*. [[cite:exp-what]] If your exact qualification is listed, you skip the college comparability assessment and don't join a college training program. [[cite:exp-skip]] **This pathway is changing fast — check the date on this page and confirm the current specialty list with the Board before relying on it.**

sections.heading: What makes it 'expedited'
sections.bodyMarkdown: On the usual [Specialist pathway](/pathways/specialist), an Australian specialist college assesses how comparable your training is — which takes time and money. The Expedited Specialist pathway removes that step for eligible specialists: **no comparability assessment and no college training program**. [[cite:exp-skip]] It was introduced following the independent (Kruk) review of regulatory settings to help suitably qualified specialists register faster. [[cite:exp-origin]]

sections.heading: Which specialties are included
sections.bodyMarkdown:
Specialties are added to the accepted-qualifications list over time. As of the verify date on this page, accepted qualifications exist for: [[cite:exp-specialties]]

- General practice
- Anaesthesia
- Obstetrics and gynaecology
- Psychiatry
- General medicine *(added 19 January 2026)*
- General paediatrics *(added 19 January 2026)*

**Diagnostic radiology** was being assessed for inclusion at the time of writing. [[cite:exp-radiology]] Because the list grows, always confirm the **current** list and your **exact** qualification on the Board's page before acting.

sections.heading: What you still need to do
sections.bodyMarkdown:
Skipping the comparability assessment doesn't mean skipping oversight. On this pathway you still complete: [[cite:exp-requirements]]

- **6 months of supervised practice**
- **Cultural safety education**
- **Orientation** to the Australian health system
- **Workplace-based assessments**

You'll hold limited or provisional registration during this period before specialist registration is granted.

sections.heading: Am I eligible?
sections.bodyMarkdown: Eligibility turns on whether your **specific specialist qualification** (from a specific country/college) appears on the accepted-qualifications list — not just your specialty. [[cite:exp-what]] The finder can suggest whether this pathway is worth investigating, but only the Board's list and your application confirm it. If your qualification isn't listed, the standard [Specialist pathway](/pathways/specialist) applies.

sections.heading: English, visas and registration
sections.bodyMarkdown: You must still meet the English language requirement [[cite:exp-english]] (see [English requirements](/english)). Roles are typically employer-sponsored, often via the Skills in Demand (subclass 482) visa [[cite:exp-visa]]. Migration is regulated — confirm with a registered MARA agent. The pathway leads to **specialist registration** with the Medical Board.

claims.text: The Expedited Specialist pathway is for SIMGs whose qualification is on the Medical Board's Expedited Specialist pathway: accepted qualifications list.
On the expedited pathway there is no comparability assessment and no college training program.
The expedited pathway was introduced/expanded following independent review of regulatory settings to speed up registration of suitably qualified specialists.
Accepted qualifications exist for general practice, anaesthesia, obstetrics and gynaecology, psychiatry, and (from 19 January 2026) general medicine and general paediatrics.
Diagnostic radiology qualifications were being assessed for inclusion as at early 2026.
The pathway requires 6 months supervised practice, cultural safety education, orientation and workplace-based assessments.
Applicants must meet the Medical Board's English language skills registration standard.
Roles are typically employer-sponsored, commonly via the Skills in Demand (subclass 482) visa.

faq.question: Is the expedited pathway open to my specialty?
faq.answerMarkdown: Only the specialties with accepted qualifications are eligible (general practice, anaesthesia, O&G, psychiatry, and from January 2026 general medicine and general paediatrics). The list is expanding, so check the Board's current page.

faq.question: Does being in an eligible specialty guarantee I qualify?
faq.answerMarkdown: No. Your *specific* qualification (country and college) must be on the accepted-qualifications list. If it isn't, you'd use the standard Specialist pathway.

faq.question: Is it really faster?
faq.answerMarkdown: It removes the college comparability assessment and training-program step, which are usually the slowest parts. You still complete 6 months of supervised practice and assessments.
